briefdescription.para: Fill color of the object, Type Property::VECTOR3.
Fill opacity of the object, Type Property::FLOAT.
Stroke color of the object, Type Property::VECTOR3.
Stroke opacity of the object, Type Property::FLOAT.
Stroke width of the object, Type Property::FLOAT.
Transform anchor of the Layer and Group object, Type Property::VECTOR2.
Transform position of the Layer and Group object, Type Property::VECTOR2.
Transform scale of the Layer and Group object, Type Property::VECTOR2 [0..100].
Transform rotation of the Layer and Group object, Type Property::FLOAT [0..360] in degrees.
Transform opacity of the Layer and Group object, Type Property::FLOAT.
Trim Start property of Shape object , value type is float [ 0 .. 100] */.
Trim End property of Shape object , value type Property::VECTOR2 [0..100] */.
UploadCompleted signal type.
Creates an initialized handle to a new VectorAnimationRenderer.

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated VectorAnimationRenderer

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use VectorAnimationRenderer::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Finalizes the renderer.
Loads the animation file.

parameterdescription.para: The url of the vector animation file

simplesect.para: True if loading success, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Loads the animation file by buffer.

parameterdescription.para: The raw buffer of the vector animation file

simplesect.para: True if loading success, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets the renderer used to display the result image.

parameterdescription.para: The renderer used to display the result image

briefdescription.para: Sets the target image size.
Renders the content to the target buffer synchronously.

parameterdescription.para: The frame number to be rendered

simplesect.para: True if the rendering success, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Notify the Renderer that rendering is stopped.
Gets the total number of frames of the file.
Gets the frame rate of the file.

simplesect.para: The frame rate of the file

briefdescription.para: Gets the default size of the file.

parameterdescription.para: The default width of the file
The default height of the file

briefdescription.para: Gets the layer information of all the child layers.
Gets the start frame and the end frame number of the composition marker.

parameterdescription.para: The composition marker of the file
The start frame number of the specified marker
The end frame number of the specified marker

simplesect.para: True if the marker is found in the file, false otherwise.

https://helpx.adobe.com/after-effects/using/layer-markers-composition-markers.html Markers exported from AfterEffect are used to describe a segment of an animation {comment/tag , startFrame, endFrame} Marker can be use to devide a resource in to separate animations by tagging the segment with comment string, start frame and duration of that segment.

briefdescription.para: Gets the all composition marker informations.
Invalidates the rendered buffer.

simplesect.para: The upload completed signal will be emitted again.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &keyPath, VectorProperty property, CallbackBase *callback, int32_t id)
memberdef.name: AddPropertyValueCallback

briefdescription.para: Sets property value for the specified keyPath. This keyPath can resolve to multiple contents. In that case, the callback's value will apply to all of them.

parameterdescription.para: The key path used to target a specific content or a set of contents that will be updated.
The callback that gets called every time the animation is rendered.
The Id to specify the callback. It should be unique and will be passed when the callback is called.

simplesect.para: A callback of the following type may be used: id The id to specify the callback. property The property that represent what you are trying to change. frameNumber The current frame number. It returns a Property::Value to set according to the property type.

detaileddescription.para: The keypath should contain object names separated by (.) and can handle globe(**) or wildchar(*). Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.

briefdescription.para: Keep the rasterized buffer to use the frame cache.

detaileddescription.para: By default, only as much as needed is kept in the rasterized buffer and not kept after use. tis API is efficient in terms of memory, but has the disadvantage of having to load the necessary buffer each time. therefore, if there are cases in which you want to improve the performance even if the app sacrifices a lot of memory, this API is useful.

briefdescription.para: Connect to this signal to be notified when the texture upload is completed.
Used for rendering a vector animation file.
